It doesn't handle untrusted data internally; it merely provides interfaces.\n\n### Score evaluations:\n- **Malware score:** All reports correctly assign `0`. The code's purpose is utility; the risk lies in how it's used (connecting to malicious contracts), not in the code itself.\n\n### Consistency:\n- All reports are consistent with each other and with the code content.\n\n---\n\n### Final assessment:\n- **Malware score:** 0 (no malicious behavior)\n- **Obfuscated score:** 0 (not obfuscated)\n- **Risk score:** 0.1 (minimal, inherent blockchain interaction risk, but not a flaw)\n\n### My own estimate:\n- **Malware:** 0\n- **Obfuscated:** 0\n- **Risk:** 0.1 (standard for blockchain interaction utilities; the main risk is external contract behavior, not the factory code itself)\n\n---\n\n**Summary:**\nThe code is a standard, autogenerated ERC20 factory with no malicious intent or vulnerabilities.
